tasks.title: Generate complete Prolog dataset for Arabic language world (Al-Andalus)
tasks.description: Create a complete Prolog world dataset at data/worlds/language/arabic/ for an Arabic language-learning world set in medieval Andalusia (Al-Andalus) — a thriving city blending Islamic, Christian, and Jewish cultures during the Golden Age. Model EVERY file after data/worlds/language/french_louisiana/ as the reference implementation. Read each french_louisiana .pl file first to match exact predicate formats. Create ALL of the following files: (1) world.pl — world/2, world_description/2, world_type (historical_medieval), game_type (language_learning), target_language (arabic), country/3, state/3, and all metadata predicates. (2) settlements.pl — at least 2 settlements with settlement/3, settlement_description/2, settlement_type/2, settlement_population/2. (3) locations.pl — at least 30 lots with lot/3, lot_type/2, lot_district/2, lot_street/2, buildings via building/3, at least 10 businesses via business/3 (souks, madrasas, hammams, caravanserais, etc.). (4) characters.pl — at least 20 characters with person/1, culturally authentic Arabic names, family trees across generations, location assignments. (5) quests.pl — at least 15 quests spanning CEFR A1-B2, covering conversation, exploration, vocabulary, grammar, cultural_knowledge types with quest objectives and prerequisite chains. (6) items.pl — at least 20 culturally specific items (calligraphy tools, spices, textiles, astrolabes, manuscripts, etc.). (7) languages.pl — Arabic language definition with language_family, script, speakers. (8) grammars.pl — at least 3 Tracery grammars including authentic Arabic name generation via grammar/2, grammar_rule/3. (9) truths.pl — at least 20 world truths covering cultural norms, social rules, linguistic notes about Arabic (formality, diglossia, right-to-left). (10) history.pl — at least 15 historical events for the world timeline. All files must be valid Prolog with snake_case atoms and single-quoted display strings.

Create ALL of the following files: (1) world.pl — world/2, world_description/2, world_type (historical_renaissance), game_type (language_learning), target_language (bengali), country/3, state/3, and all metadata predicates. (2) settlements.pl — at least 2 settlements with culturally authentic names. (3) locations.pl — at least 30 lots with buildings and at least 10 businesses (textile workshops, riverside ghats, bazaars, temples, etc.). (4) characters.pl — at least 20 characters with authentic Bengali names, family trees, location assignments. (5) quests.pl — at least 15 quests spanning CEFR A1-B2. (6) items.pl — at least 20 culturally specific items (muslin cloth, terracotta pots, bamboo crafts, spices, literary manuscripts, etc.). (7) languages.pl — Bengali language definition. (8) grammars.pl — at least 3 Tracery grammars with authentic Bengali name generation. (9) truths.pl — at least 20 world truths about Bengali culture, social customs, and linguistic features. (10) history.pl — at least 15 historical events. All files must be valid Prolog.

tasks.title: Generate complete Prolog dataset for Creole Colonial world
tasks.description: Expand the existing data/worlds/creole_colonial/world.pl into a complete Prolog world dataset. Read the existing world.pl first to understand the setting, then read data/worlds/language/french_louisiana/ for predicate format reference. Create ALL missing files: (1) Expand world.pl if needed — ensure it has country/3, state/3 definitions. (2) settlements.pl — at least 2 settlements. (8) truths.pl — at least 15 world truths establishing cultural norms, social rules, world lore. (9) history.pl — at least 10 historical events establishing world backstory. (10) rules.pl — at least 10 world-specific volition rules extending data/worlds/base/rules/. (11) actions.pl — at least 10 world-specific actions extending data/worlds/base/actions/. (12) narratives.pl — at least 5 narrative templates with multi-stage story structures. All files must be valid Prolog.
